title: Phymatosorus scolopendria
taxonomy: Phymatosorus scolopendria is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It is a type of fern, classified under the phylum Pteridophyta. This plant is native to tropical regions of Asia and is also known as the "centipede fern" due to its unique, long-stemmed fronds. Phymatosorus scolopendria is further divided into various subspecies based on its geographical distribution and physical characteristics. The species is widely cultivated as an ornamental plant in various parts of the world.
uses: Phymatosorus scolopendria, also known as the resurrection fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional Asian medicine, Phymatosorus scolopendria is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases. Some studies have also investigated the plant's potential in treating cancer and other diseases. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Phymatosorus scolopendria as a medicinal plant.
